subject: Sociology
title: Refugees and Forced Migration
description: A quick, practical guide to Refugees and Forced Migration.

hook.text: 100 million people are forcibly displaced worldwide. That's more than the population of most European countries.

buildup.text: Refugees flee persecution, war, and violence. Internally displaced people flee but remain within their own country.

discovery.text: The 1951 Refugee Convention guarantees protection, but many nations restrict entry and detain asylum seekers.

twist.text: Most refugees are hosted by developing nations, not wealthy ones. The poorest countries carry the heaviest burden.

climax.text: Forced migration reveals global inequality at its starkest: the powerful create crises; the powerless flee them.

punchline.text: Nobody chooses to be a refugee.

quiz.question: Which countries host the most refugees?
